w20: Compute-optimized x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, providing efficient dedicated processing for general-purpose cloud workloads and application hosting.
c150: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, designed for efficient general-purpose workloads.
c200: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is an x86_64 server featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. This compute-focused instance utilizes AMD EPYC 7B13 processors for reliable general-purpose cloud computing tasks.
w150: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-2 is a compute-optimized instance built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs powered by a single physical core with 2 threads per core using the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. The system includes 8 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core. This configuration does not include a GPU or local storage. Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operation throughput are categorized as weak or poor relative to broader infrastructure standards. The server supports AVX, AES, and SHA-NI instruction sets for cryptographic and vector operations. With an on-demand price of 0.0599 USD per hour, this instance is designed for general-purpose computing tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU resources without requiring intensive multi-threaded processing or high-bandwidth memory access. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, basic CI/CD pipelines, and standard application hosting.
